productStatement: GrantLayer turns agentic grant workflows into verifiable institutional records.

packageComponents.name: Application package
packageComponents.purpose: Runnable GrantLayer application code, dependencies, and entry points
packageComponents.notes: Must be built from validated source with reproducible build steps. No debug or test code in production artifact.

packageComponents.name: Configuration schema
packageComponents.purpose: Validated schema that defines required, optional, and per-runtime-mode configuration fields
packageComponents.notes: Must be explicit and versioned. Production must refuse to start if required fields are missing or invalid.

packageComponents.name: Runtime mode declaration
packageComponents.purpose: Declared runtime mode validated on startup
packageComponents.notes: Must be explicitly set and validated. Must fail closed if undeclared or invalid.

packageComponents.name: Database / persistence configuration
packageComponents.purpose: Active persistence backend and connection configuration
packageComponents.notes: SQLite acceptable locally. Production requires explicit durable backend with validated connection.

packageComponents.name: Secret source configuration
packageComponents.purpose: How keys, tokens, and credentials are sourced, rotated, and stored
packageComponents.notes: Production requires explicit secret manager. No demo or placeholder secrets allowed.

packageComponents.name: Auth / operator access configuration
packageComponents.purpose: Active auth provider and operator access model
packageComponents.notes: Production requires explicit auth provider and validated operator bootstrap.

packageComponents.name: Evidence storage configuration
packageComponents.purpose: Where evidence bundles are persisted and retrieved
packageComponents.notes: Production requires durable, explicit storage. Credentials must not leak into evidence metadata.

packageComponents.name: Policy / rule pack configuration
packageComponents.purpose: Which policy rules are active and where they are loaded from
packageComponents.notes: Production must not load demo or unverified rule packs.

packageComponents.name: Observability / logging configuration
packageComponents.purpose: Structured logging, metrics, alerting, and tracing backends and thresholds
packageComponents.notes: Production requires explicit structured logging and metrics. Sink credentials must not appear in startup output.

packageComponents.name: Backup / restore configuration
packageComponents.purpose: Automated backup schedule, retention policy, point-in-time recovery, and disaster runbooks
packageComponents.notes: Production requires explicit tested backup/restore plan. Credentials must be scoped to backup operations only.

packageComponents.name: Health / readiness checks
packageComponents.purpose: Health and readiness probes verifying application and dependency status
packageComponents.notes: Must be implemented in production. Must not leak secrets or sensitive configuration in responses.

packageComponents.name: API / OpenAPI contract artifact
packageComponents.purpose: Versioned OpenAPI specification defining the API contract
packageComponents.notes: Must match deployed application version. Must be available to integrators.

packageComponents.name: Demo / sample data bundle
packageComponents.purpose: Deterministic fixtures, example grants, evidence bundles, and policy rules for walkthroughs
packageComponents.notes: Must not be loaded in production. Must be labeled as non-production.

packageComponents.name: Integration example bundle
packageComponents.purpose: Example API calls, SDK snippets, and partner integration patterns
packageComponents.notes: Must not contain real credentials or secrets. Must be labeled as examples only.

packageComponents.name: Operator runbook
packageComponents.purpose: Step-by-step instructions for common operator tasks
packageComponents.notes: Must be specific to the target environment. Must not contain secrets.

packageComponents.name: Upgrade / migration notes
packageComponents.purpose: Documented steps and compatibility expectations between versions
packageComponents.notes: Must be deterministic and tested. Must include rollback guidance.

packageComponents.name: Extension / adaptor configuration
packageComponents.purpose: Which extensions, adapters, and optional integrations are enabled
packageComponents.notes: Must be explicit in production. Must not load unverified or unauthorized extensions.

packageComponents.name: Production hardening checklist
packageComponents.purpose: Pre-deployment verification list covering deployment-critical configuration
packageComponents.notes: Must be completed and signed off before any production claim.

deploymentBoundaries.boundary: Product Core should not depend on one hosting platform
deploymentBoundaries.rationale: The core should run wherever the runtime configuration boundary is satisfied. Docker, Kubernetes, cloud, and bare-metal are all potential future targets.

deploymentBoundaries.boundary: Docker / Kubernetes / cloud deployment may be future packaging options, not Product Core assumptions
deploymentBoundaries.rationale: Future packaging layers may wrap the product package, but the Product Core must not require them.

deploymentBoundaries.boundary: Deployment-specific configuration should stay outside Product Core records
deploymentBoundaries.rationale: Host names, container IDs, and infrastructure metadata should not appear in evidence bundles, audit records, or provenance records.

deploymentBoundaries.boundary: Secrets must not be baked into images, docs, examples, fixtures, or logs
deploymentBoundaries.rationale: The build process must not embed credentials into artifacts.

deploymentBoundaries.boundary: Extension / adaptor configuration should be environment-specific
deploymentBoundaries.rationale: Which adapters are active should be part of runtime configuration, not hard-coded into the product package.

deploymentBoundaries.boundary: Production mode should fail closed if deployment-critical configuration is missing
deploymentBoundaries.rationale: Missing, invalid, or unvalidated deployment configuration must block startup.

referencePackageExpectations: Runnable application package with validated build artifact or reproducible source build steps
Example non-production configuration with explicit non-production warning labels
Versioned OpenAPI contract artifact matching the deployed application
Example rule packs with deterministic policy requirements, exclusions, and permission profiles
Example evidence fixtures with structured evidence bundles and verification examples
Dry-run smoke script as a lightweight executable confidence check
Operator runbook with common operations, step-by-step instructions, and rollback guidance
Production hardening checklist covering runtime mode, secrets, auth, persistence, observability, backup/restore, and network exposure
Explicit non-production warning labels on every example, fixture, and demo configuration
productionRequiredConfiguration: Explicit runtime mode set to production
Explicit database/persistence backend with validated reachability
Explicit secret source configuration with validated values
Explicit auth/operator access configuration with validated role mapping
Explicit evidence storage configuration with durable retention
Explicit observability/logging configuration with structured output
Explicit backup/restore plan with tested recovery procedures
Explicit network binding/exposure rules with TLS and access controls
Explicit health/readiness checks implemented and accurate
Explicit production hardening checklist completed and signed off
Fail-closed behavior for missing or invalid required configuration
